2604.07749.title: Beyond Social Pressure: Benchmarking Epistemic Attack in Large Language Models
2604.07749.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) can shift their answers under pressure in ways that reflect accommodation rather than reasoning. Prior work on sycophancy has focused mainly on disagreement, flattery, and preference alignment, leaving a broader set of epistemic failures less explored. We introduce \textbf{PPT-Bench}, a diagnostic benchmark for evaluating \textit{epistemic attack}, where prompts challenge the legitimacy of knowledge, values, or identity rather than simply opposing a previous answer. PPT-Bench is organized around the Philosophical Pressure Taxonomy (PPT), which defines four types of philosophical pressure: Epistemic Destabilization, Value Nullification, Authority Inversion, and Identity Dissolution. Each item is tested at three layers: a baseline prompt (L0), a single-turn pressure condition (L1), and a multi-turn Socratic escalation (L2). This allows us to measure epistemic inconsistency between L0 and L1, and conversational capitulation in L2. Across five models, these pressure types produce statistically separable inconsistency patterns, suggesting that epistemic attack exposes weaknesses not captured by standard social-pressure benchmarks. Mitigation results are strongly type- and model-dependent: prompt-level anchoring and persona-stability prompts perform best in API settings, while Leading Query Contrastive Decoding is the most reliable intervention for open models.
2604.07749.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在壓力下可以以反映適應而非推理的方式改變其回答。先前對於諂媚的研究主要集中在分歧、恭維和偏好對齊上，留下了一組更廣泛的認識失誤未被深入探討。我們介紹了\textbf{PPT-Bench}，這是一個用於評估\textit{認識攻擊}的診斷基準，其中提示挑戰知識、價值或身份的合法性，而不僅僅是反對先前的回答。PPT-Bench圍繞哲學壓力分類法（PPT）組織，該分類法定義了四種類型的哲學壓力：認識不穩定、價值無效化、權威顛倒和身份溶解。每個項目在三個層次上進行測試：基線提示（L0）、單回合壓力條件（L1）和多回合蘇格拉底升級（L2）。這使我們能夠測量L0和L1之間的認識不一致性，以及L2中的對話屈服。在五個模型中，這些壓力類型產生了統計上可分離的不一致性模式，表明認識攻擊暴露了標準社會壓力基準未捕捉到的弱點。減輕結果強烈依賴於類型和模型：提示級別的錨定和角色穩定提示在API設置中表現最佳，而引導查詢對比解碼是開放模型中最可靠的干預措施。
2604.07749.author: Steven Au et.al.
